rag_rules: Place generated AGREE annex blocks in the target component or implementation scope, following AADL scope rules
Preserve existing AADL architecture by inserting annex blocks in valid locations without modifying other elements
Use visible identifiers when available, and omit implementation-only subcomponent paths in component type annexes
Place input envelope assumptions and output range guarantees in component type annexes when concrete bounds or visible relations are available
generic_rules: Insert annex blocks in the most specific valid scope to maintain architectural clarity
Avoid using implementation-only subcomponent paths in component type annexes
Use AGREE scalar types (int, real, bool) for declarations and comparisons
Ensure helper declaration types align with the expression types in comparisons
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: Use initialized temporal recurrence for stateful equations, e.g., `0.0 -> pre(t) + step`
Use nested `if ... then ... else` for branching logic in AGREE contracts
Use AGREE logical operators (and, or, not, =>) for contract expressions
anti_patterns: Do not use implementation-only subcomponent paths in component type annexes
Avoid range comparisons or 0/1 equality over Boolean, enumeration-like, or opaque data ports
Do not convert Boolean domain values into integers in AGREE contracts
irrelevant_topics: AGREE validator diagnostics and repair patterns
AGREE annex placement for assumptions and guarantees
